name: Dutch B2 Vocabulary
description: Upper-intermediate Dutch. Understands most spoken and written Dutch. Can argue and explain.

facts.explanation: artikel — article (report). Also: article (item, wares), article (section of a legal document), article
beeld — image. Also: statue, sculpture, impression, idea
begrip — understanding, comprehension. Also: understanding, empathy, notion, concept, term, institution (well-known entity)
bepalen — to determine, to figure out. Also: to determine, to decide, to choose, to limit oneself, to restrain oneself, to contain oneself
beschrijven — to write on. Also: to write (a letter) to, to describe
bron — source. Also: spring (in fountain), well (the place to get water)
centraal — central, being in the centre.
conclusie — conclusion. Also: opinion (judicial opinion delivered by an Advocate General to the European Court of Justice for a legal solution in a case)
conflict — a conflict, clash or dispute.
creëren — to create, make, put into existence. Also: to design creatively, to appoint, invest with an office etc., to establish, erect, found, to cause, be responsible for
deelnemen — to take part, to partake, to participate.
dienen — to serve. Also: to ought to, to be to, be expected to

directeur — director, manager (in a company), general manager, CEO (or at a lower (e.g., branch) level). Also: director (a person in charge of various other institutions), director or manager (who has an executive mandate within an institution, e.g., for HR)
doel — aim, purpose. Also: destination, goal
festival — a festival (festive event or gathering).
functie — job, position. Also: function, purpose, function, use, purpose (of a machine or tool)
game — a video game, an electronic game.
gamer — a gamer, someone who plays video games.
geschiedenis — history (that which happened in the past, especially insofar as it is relevant for later times). Also: history, historical science (the scientific study thereof), event, happening
grof — rough, coarse. Also: rude, very blunt, extreme, gross, grave
handel — trade (activity of trading). Also: business, company (commercial entity), shop, store (merchant's business), goods, things, any collective or collection; stuff, lot, crowd, whole shebang
immers — Indicates that an explanation is being given for an earlier statement.; indeed, after all.
keuze — choice, whether already made or not.

markt — a physical market place, usually a public square. Also: The whole of trade, commercial activity, any specific economic sector where competitive trade occurs in goods or services, a single, regular trade event in one (or more coordinated) places, usually in one town; general or sector-specific, the canopy which covers a food market; the roof structure of a market hall

punt — point (a position, place, or spot). Also: point (moment in time), point (central idea, argument, or opinion of a discussion or presentation), point (tally of worth or score, such as in a game), point (mark, note, or grade, as in for a class), point
sfeer — sphere, round object. Also: atmosphere, ambiance, mood
stelling — position, configuration. Also: thesis, contention, sentence, theorem, scaffold, shelving unit
verband — a bandage. Also: a link or relation
verklaren — to declare, state. Also: to explain, to clarify
verplichten — to oblige, compel, force. Also: to affiliate, to unite
vestigen — to establish, to place. Also: to establish oneself, to settle
vloeken — to curse, to damn, invoke a negative spell. Also: to blaspheme, curse, swear, to clash, go very badly with
voorbeeld — example. Also: role model, preview
vorm — a form, shape. Also: a format
wetenschappelijk — scientific (of or having to do with science).

questionTemplates.questionFormat: Which word is closest in meaning to '{targetLanguageWord}'?
